message.content: {"items":[{"title":"German Federal Election 2021 (GLES 2021) – Rolling Cross-Section Campaign Survey (Pre- and Post-election)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.14075","justification":"Contains detailed individual-level data on political behaviour, vote choice, campaign effects, and political attitudes in the 2021 federal election in Germany, fitting the topics of political behaviour, elections, and government/political systems in the 2020s."},{"title":"German Longitudinal Election Study (GLES) 2021 – Pre-election Cross-Section","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.14076","justification":"Focuses on voting behaviour, party preferences, and attitudes toward political institutions and democracy in Germany immediately before the 2021 federal election, directly addressing political behaviour and elections in the 2020s."},{"title":"German Longitudinal Election Study (GLES) 2021 – Post-election Cross-Section","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.14077","justification":"Provides representative post-election data on turnout, vote choice, coalition preferences, and evaluations of government and political actors for Germany’s 2021 federal election, matching the requested themes and period."},{"title":"German Federal Election 2021 – GLES 2021 Campaign Panel Survey","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.14126","justification":"Panel survey tracing individuals over the 2021 federal election campaign in Germany, with rich measures of political attitudes, participation, and reactions to parties and government, squarely about political behaviour and elections in the 2020s."},{"title":"GLES 2021 – Short-term Campaign Panel (Corona Panel Waves on the 2021 Federal Election)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.14107","justification":"Examines political behaviour and attitudes during the COVID-19 context surrounding the 2021 German federal election, including evaluations of government performance and electoral choices, in Germany in the 2020s."},{"title":"German Longitudinal Election Study (GLES) – 2020 Corona Study, Waves on Political Attitudes and Behaviour","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.13560","justification":"COVID-19-related panel survey in Germany starting 2020, with repeated measures of trust in government, satisfaction with democracy, and political behaviour, directly related to political attitudes and government in the 2020s."},{"title":"GLES 2021 – Online Nonprobability Survey on the Federal Election","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.14080","justification":"Nonprobability online sample for the 2021 German federal election including vote intention, participation, and attitudes toward parties and political institutions, relevant to political behaviour and elections in Germany during the 2020s."},{"title":"German Federal Election 2021 – GLES 2021 Candidate Survey","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.14078","justification":"Survey of candidates in the 2021 federal election in Germany, covering their political attitudes, positions, and views on parties and parliament, relating to government and political systems and the electoral process in the 2020s."},{"title":"German Federal Election 2021 – GLES 2021 Constituency-Level Data","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.14079","justification":"Aggregated data at electoral district level for the 2021 German federal election, including vote shares, turnout, and contextual indicators, suitable for studying electoral behaviour and party system dynamics in the 2020s."},{"title":"State Parliament Election in Baden-Württemberg 2021 – Pre- and Post-election Study","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.14122","justification":"Covers individual political behaviour, vote choice, and attitudes toward state government and parties for the 2021 Baden-Württemberg state election in Germany, aligning with political behaviour, government, and elections in the 2020s."}]}
